title: Find a peak element
hints: Check the corner elements first - they only need to be compared with one neighbor.
Use binary search to find the peak in the middle elements.
If A[mid] is a peak (greater than or equal to both neighbors), return it.
If A[mid] > A[mid-1], the peak must be on the right side (move lo = mid + 1).
Otherwise, the peak must be on the left side (move hi = mid - 1).
The time complexity should be O(log N).
